commands.repair_reason: Protected labels, broad architecture scope, missing real behavior proof, dirty mergeability, and security-state blockers require maintainer/security review rather than an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The patch still raises concrete security-state rollback concerns in delayed legacy imports for tokens, approvals, pairing, and plugin state.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve live device-auth tokens on legacy i... commands.review_summary:
The PR moves OpenClaw runtime/session/transcript/plugin/channel/scheduler/artifact state from scattered file-backed stores into shared and per-agent SQLite storage, with migrations, SDK/protocol/UI/docs/tests updates.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blockers by source/discussion evidence: the migration paths write legacy snapshots into live SQLite-backed stores that runtime also mutates, so delayed doctor runs can roll state backward. A live upgraded-runtime repro was not established in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected labels, broad architecture scope, missing real behavior proof, dirty mergeability, and security-state blockers require maintainer/security review rather than an automated repair lane.
